uses: Unfortunately, I couldn't find any information about the plant Cracca polysperma. However, I can suggest that you may be referring to the plant Crataegus monogyna, also known as Cracca monogyna or Hawthorn, which is known for its medicinal uses. Crataegus monogyna has been traditionally used to treat cardiovascular issues, such as high blood pressure and heart failure. The flowers and leaves of the plant are rich in flavonoids, which are believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
